lab.title: Conditional Statements Practice Lab
lab.intro: This lab will help you practice writing and understanding conditional statements in Python. We'll start with basic if statements and progress to nested conditionals.
lab.tutor_opening: Let's practice conditionals using real-world examples. I'll show you a concept, then you'll try it yourself. Let's begin!

cells.title: Conditional Statements Overview
cells.narrative: Conditionals let programs make decisions. The basic structure is: if [condition]: [code to execute]. Add else: for alternative paths.
cells.tutor_script: Conditionals execute code only when specific conditions are met. Let's see a simple example.

cells.title: Basic If Statement
cells.tutor_script: This code checks temperature and prints different messages. Try modifying the temperature value to see how the output changes.

cells.title: Number Comparison Task
cells.task_prompt: Write a program that checks if a number is positive, negative, or zero. Use if-elif-else structure.
cells.starter_code:
num = -5

# Your code here
if num > 0:
    print("Positive")
# Add elif and else conditions
tips: Start with if for the first condition
Use elif for the second condition
End with else for the default case

cells.title: Nested Conditionals Example
cells.tutor_script: Nested conditionals let us check multiple requirements. The program verifies age AND subscription status.

cells.title: Order Validation Challenge
cells.task_prompt: Create a program that validates an order: check if stock is available and if the customer has sufficient points.
success_criteria: Checks both stock and points conditions
tips: Print different messages for stock vs points issues
Test edge cases like zero values

lab.completion_message: Great work! You've practiced basic conditionals, if-elif-else chains, and nested conditionals. Try experimenting with different values to see how the logic behaves.
